title: Achankovil Sastha Temple
tagline: Achankovil Sastha Temple — spiritual town of Kerala

overview.short: The Achankovil Sastha Temple is a Hindu temple located near the banks of the Achankovil River in the Kollam district in the Indian state of Kerala.
overview.description: The Achankovil Sastha Temple is a Hindu temple located near the banks of the Achankovil River in the Kollam district in the Indian state of Kerala. It is one of the five important temples dedicated to Shasta in Kerala, the others being Kulathupuzha, Aryankavu, Sabarimala and Kanthamala. The idol in the temple is believed to have been consecrated by Parashurama. The temple is known for curing poisonous snakebites.

topPlaces.name: Kumbhavurutty Waterfalls
topPlaces.description: Kumbhavurutty Waterfall is a famous waterfalls in South India situated near Aryankavu panchayath in Kollam district of Kerala near Tamil Nadu border. It is located 6.5 km away from Achenkovil.

topPlaces.name: Manalar Waterfalls
topPlaces.description: Manalar Waterfalls is situated at Achankovil in Kollam district of Kerala. It is on the eastern area of Kollam District and Konni forest.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Achankovil Sastha Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Achankovil Sastha Temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Achankovil Sastha Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Thiruvananthapuram International Airport (71 km) and the nearest railway station is Thiruvananthapuram Central (70 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Achankovil Sastha Temple?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Achankovil Sastha Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Achankovil Sastha Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Achankovil Sastha Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Achankovil Sastha Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Kumbhavurutty Waterfalls, Manalar Waterfalls.
